question: Which of the following is the correct declaration of an abstract class?

An abstract class must be defined with the keyword abstract.

question: The variables, methods and inner classes that are declared ………………… are accessible to the sub-classes of the class in which they are declared.
explanation: A protected keyword makes the member of a class accessible in current class and all its direct and indirect subclasses.

question: What is the package in which System class is defined in Java?
explanation:
java.lang contains the classes and interfaces which are essential to Java language. 

The following are some of the classes defined in java.lang package
Object
Thread
String
Wrapper classes for primitive types
Math 
System

As these classes are essential to all Java programs, the java.lang package is imported implicitly by every Java source file.

question: Which of the following exceptions may be thrown by read() method of Reader class?

Java methods declared in an interface can only be public. By default they are public.

question: A class can ------ an interface
A Java interface can contain ---------
options: both public abstract methods and public static final variables.
explanation:
Prior to Java 8, an interface could contain only abstract methods and final variables.

But since Java 8, an interface can also contain default methods and static methods. Both of these methods will have implementations in the interface.

question: What is the correct way of defining a class ABC which uses an interface DEF?
Which of the following statements are correct?
options: A class can extend multiple classes
A class can implement multiple interfaces
An interface can not be extended
explanation:
In Java a class can extend only a single class. But it can implement multiple interfaces. 

And an interface can extend another interface.

question: The default value of a static integer variable of a class in Java is,
explanation:
Default value for static variables is 0 for numbers and null for objects.

Default values for instance variables is also 0/null

question: Which statement is not true in java language?
options: A public member of a class can be accessed in all the packages.
A private member of a class can be accessed by all classes in the same package.
A private member of a class can not be accessed from its derived class.
A protected member of a class can be accessed from its derived class.
explanation: A private member of a class can be accessed by only methods of the same class.

question: A method is declared as ---- to stop derived classes from overriding this method.
explanation: A final method can not be overridden by sub-classes

question: Which of the following statements is not true about abstract classes in Java
options: A class containing abstract methods is called an abstract class.
Abstract methods should be implemented in the derived class.
An abstract class cannot have non-abstract methods.
A class must be qualified as ‘abstract’ class, if it contains one or more abstract methods.

question: Which of the following is not true?
options: An interface can extend another interface.
A class which is implementing an interface must implement all the methods of the interface.

question: Which of the following statements regarding the keyword "finally" is true?
options: A finally block is executed before the catch block but after the try block.
A finally block is executed, only after the catch block is executed.

A finally block is executed, only if an exception occurs.

question: What is Java byte code?
options: A code generated by Java compiler
Another name for java source file
explanation:
Java compiler creates machine independant java byte code. 

Then Java Virtual Machine translates this to the corresponding machine language instructions and executes them.

Java byte code is the one which makes a Java language architecture independant.

question: Which method is automatically called by Java system before an object is garbage collected?
explanation:
If a class has defined finalize() method, then before the object is garbage collected, this method is called. So finalize method can be used to clean up the object. 

But since we are not sure when an object will be garbage collected, we can not rely on this method.

question: What is the correct order for specifying differnt parts in a Java program?

question: All exception types are subclasses of the built-in class  -----
explanation: The Throwable class is the superclass of all errors and exceptions in the Java language. Only objects that are instances of this class (or one of its subclasses) are thrown by the Java Virtual Machine or can be thrown by the Java throw statement. Similarly, only this class or one of its subclasses can be the argument type in a catch clause.

question: When an overridden method is called from a subclass , it calls -----
options: Compiler decides which method to call

question: What is the range of short data type in Java?

explanation: In any arithmetic expressions, byte,short or char values are automatically promoted to an int and the result is also an int.

question: Which of the following is Long literal?
explanation: An integer literal suffixed with L or l is a long literal. If there is no L/l suffix, then it is an integer.

question: What is the name for combining methods and attributes into classes?
Which of the following methods of Thread class waits for a  thread to die?
explanation:
When we invoke the join() method on a thread, the calling thread goes into a waiting state until the thread specified ends.

e.g.

  MyThread th1 =new MyThread();
  th1.start();
  ..
  ..
 ..
  th1.join();//line 1

Now line 1 will pause the main thread, until th1 is terminated.

question: What will happen if we directly call the run method of a thread instead of start() method?
options: The method will run in separate thread
The method will run in calling thread
explanation:
If we call start method, a new thread is created and then the run() method is executed in this new thread.

But if we call run method directly from calling thread, then new thread is not created and method is run from calling thread only

question: Why does the following code does not compile?
qncode:
int a = 10;
byte b = a;
options: There is no byte data type in Java
Implicit conversion from higher precision to lower precision is not allowed
explanation:
Automatic conversion can be done when we are widening the data type or converting to higher precision.

 But if we convert from higher precision data type to lower precision, then automatic conversion is not possible because there may be loss of accuracy. There will be a syntax error in such cases. 

But such conversions can be done by using explicit type casting

e.g.
byte b = (byte)a;

question: What is the output of the following program?

When a smaller value is assigned to a more precise variable, the implicit conversion takes place. (widening conversion).

s = m;//error

 If you do not want it to be abstract, you should use keyword default or static with it. defaylt or static methods of an interface must have implementations.

question: How do you combine two strings?
explanation:
Two strings can be joined using + operator.

e.g.
String s1 = "hello";
String s2 = s1+" world";//s2 is hello world

Strings can also be joined using .concat method

String s3 = s1.concat(" world");//s3 is hello world

question: To find the square root of a number in a Java program, you should do the following.
explanation:
To use a method from another package, you can either use a fully qualified name or you can import the required package. 

Using Math.sqrt() indicates that use the static sqrt() method from the package Math.

You can import the sqrt() method using import static java.lang.Math.sqrt;

You can import the entire Math class also.

question: Why does the following code  does not compile?
options: FileNotFoundException must come earlier than IO Exception
try block can't be followed by multiple catch blocks
try block should always be followed by a catch and a finally blocks
explanation:
FileNotFoundException is a subclass of IOException. If we write IOException handler earlier, that handler will catch FileNotFoundException too.  

That is why the java compiler prevents you from writing superclass exception before subclass exception.
